policy.sourceRule: Gaps are derived from active-mission.md and from operational observations.
policy.linkRule: When a Discovery candidate addresses a known gap, tag the candidate with the gap_id.
policy.reviewRule: Review this registry whenever active-mission.md is updated.

gaps.description: Canonical Directive engine surface is only partially materialized as one reusable executable core with clear lane boundaries and host-adapter seams
gaps.current_state: A first engine slice exists, but real source intake still falls back to host helpers, Markdown-first assets, and manual post-decision lane handoff
gaps.desired_state: Engine owns substantially more intake, routing, adaptation/improvement, proof, decision, and handoff state while Discovery, Forge, and Architecture operate as clear Engine lanes

gaps.description: Discovery has no machine-readable intake queue for programmatic query or worker-pull
gaps.desired_state: Shadow intake queue alongside markdown with schema validation
gaps.resolution_notes: Shadow intake queue created at discovery/intake-queue.json

gaps.description: Mission-conditioned usefulness is not operationalized as a machine-readable input
gaps.current_state: Usefulness evaluated by human judgment only
gaps.desired_state: Active-mission artifact referenced in Discovery templates
gaps.resolution_notes: Active-mission artifact created at knowledge/active-mission.md; fast-path template updated with mission-alignment fields

gaps.description: Behavior-preserving transformation has zero operational materialization despite being doctrine first-class
gaps.current_state: No templates, schemas, evaluators, or promotion profiles for transformation work
gaps.desired_state: Transformation record template, proof schema, evaluator contract, and promotion profile
gaps.resolution_notes: Transformation record template, proof schema, evaluator contract, and promotion profile created

gaps.description: Verifier lanes are gate-shaped (binary pass/fail) but not evaluator-shaped (dimensional measurement)
gaps.current_state: npm run check:* scripts provide pass/fail only
gaps.desired_state: Evaluator contract defining metric, baseline, measurement method, threshold, comparison mode

gaps.description: OpenClaw-to-Discovery coordination is implicit, not contractual
gaps.current_state: OpenClaw-root helpers can append pending candidates into the primary Discovery queue through direct submission, stale verification freshness signaling, and maintenance/watchdog degraded-state signaling. The first two bounded paths are exercised; the third is implemented and checker-backed.
gaps.desired_state: Formal contract plus exercised bounded submission paths from OpenClaw root into Discovery intake.
gaps.resolution_notes: Root helpers at scripts/submit-openclaw-discovery-candidate.ps1, scripts/submit-openclaw-runtime-verification-signal.ps1, and scripts/submit-openclaw-maintenance-watchdog-signal.ps1 plus their Mission Control checkers are active. Candidates dw-openclaw-discovery-submission-flow and dw-openclaw-runtime-verification-freshness-2026-03-22 exercised the first two paths; dw-openclaw-maintenance-watchdog-signal-lane materialized the third lane without fabricating a live incident.

gaps.description: Monitor/defer review cadence has no automated enforcement
gaps.current_state: Monitor records have review dates but no checker flags overdue items
gaps.desired_state: Checker script scanning monitor records and flagging overdue reviews

gaps.description: Discovery intake queue has only 1 entry vs 53 routing records; shadow mode cannot prove itself toward 10-candidate transition threshold
gaps.current_state: Shadow queue with 1 completed entry; transition requires 10 candidates
gaps.desired_state: At least 10 candidates processed through both markdown and queue
gaps.resolution_notes: Backfilled 9 recent routing records into queue (total 10 entries, all valid). Queue has reached the 10-candidate transition threshold. Shadow-to-primary transition now eligible pending operator approval.

gaps.description: Discovery-intake-queue contract promises a validation checker but none exists in Mission Control scripts
gaps.current_state: No checker script validates queue entries against schema or checks markdown linkage
gaps.desired_state: Checker that validates queue entries against schema, checks markdown forward/backward linkage, and reports sync gaps
gaps.resolution_notes: Checker created at mission-control/scripts/check-discovery-intake-queue.ts; registered as npm run check:discovery-intake-queue

gaps.description: Architecture-to-Forge handoff contract has never been exercised in practice
gaps.current_state: Contract exists at shared/contracts/architecture-to-forge.md but no record shows it being used
gaps.desired_state: At least one Architecture candidate handed off to Forge through the contract
gaps.resolution_notes: Formal handoff record created at forge/handoff/2026-03-22-autoresearch-architecture-to-forge-handoff.md. Exercises all required contract fields. Real work (Architecture extraction → Forge bounded run → promotion) completed 2026-03-19–2026-03-20.

gaps.description: Evaluator contract exists but no candidate has used a real dimensional evaluator with numeric before/after comparison
gaps.current_state: Pilot transformation used pass/fail checks (forge-sync, typecheck, build), not dimensional measurement
gaps.desired_state: At least one candidate uses a dimensional evaluator (latency, cost, memory, or throughput with numeric baseline and result)
gaps.resolution_notes: Backend test boilerplate consolidation uses dimensional evaluator with numeric before/after: 1,279→1,016 lines (-20.6%), 47,603→39,572 bytes (-16.9%). Both exceed 10% threshold. First non-vacuous dimensional evaluator proof.

gaps.description: Discovery-first intake coverage still trails routing history; the front door needs more native upstream usage and less historical backfill
gaps.current_state: Discovery queue is in primary mode with explicit intake linkage for split-case entries, and executable coverage analysis now measures routine usage from the live corpus instead of hand-counted markdown totals.
gaps.desired_state: All new candidates enter through Discovery queue plus markdown intake; intake coverage grows over time instead of relying on backfill
gaps.resolution_notes: Executable checker `npm run check:discovery-front-door-coverage` now measures front-door routine usage from the live corpus. Current metrics: queue in primary mode, 22 total entries, 14 post-primary entries, 13 native-like post-primary entries, and 100% native-like post-primary intake linkage coverage. The lone post-primary missing intake link is a backfill candidate (`al-parked-cli-anything`), so Discovery-first routine usage is confirmed for native work.

gaps.description: Root-level external tool bridge behavior still preserves agent-lab-era assumptions outside the Directive Workspace ownership model
gaps.related_mission_objective: Replaceable integrations and clean product ownership
gaps.current_state: OpenClaw-owned external-tool bridge now resolves through root adapters, current Forge source packs, and Mission Control runtime dependencies where applicable
gaps.desired_state: No live runtime path depends on workspace/agent-lab internals; remaining historical references are archival only
gaps.resolution_notes: Root runner migrated to scripts/invoke-external-tool.ps1 + scripts/external-tools/*; root writeback helper restored; live adapter paths no longer point to workspace/agent-lab/orchestration

gaps.description: Discovery intake queue has limited primary-mode operational history; needs 5+ native entries to confirm stability
gaps.desired_state: 5+ candidates processed natively through primary-mode queue
gaps.resolution_notes: 5 native primary-mode queue entries achieved. Three BPT candidates processed end-to-end through native queue.
